text.title: design-docs-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-docs-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Treating an LLM-generated design doc as authoritative without a human review pass. LLMs produce confident-sounding text that may be missing domain-specific constraints, organizational context, or real trade-offs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs pattern-match to generic design doc templates and fill in plausible-sounding content. They lack knowledge of your specific system constraints, team norms, and organizational history. A human review pass is mandatory to catch incorrect assumptions before the doc influences implementation.

failure-mode.description: Sending a design doc to stakeholders without a human reviewing the LLM-generated content for accuracy. LLMs produce confident text that may contain incorrect assumptions about system constraints, organizational context, or technical feasibility.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM-generated docs sound authoritative while potentially missing the domain knowledge that makes a design doc valuable. A human review pass is the only way to catch incorrect assumptions before they are baked into the implementation.
